content.problemStatement:
If you are here - then you should already know the basic syntax of C++.

If not, then head to the [Learn C++ course](https://www.codechef.com/learn/course/cpp) and finish it once.

Let's now take it up a notch and train you to solve programming problems. Before that, let us do a quick recap of what we had learned about C++ in [Learn C++ Path](https://www.codechef.com/learn/course/cpp).

On CodeChef and other platforms, you will come across programming problems.
- Each programming problem has a **problem statement**
- You write a **solution code** which will solve the problem
- Your code is run, does some computation and generates **your output**
- **Your output** is compared with the **Expected output**. 
  - If they match - then your code is correct!
  - If they don't match - then there is some error in your code.

Click on **Next** to proceed!
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
